thesis: Quanta Services is the dominant North American infrastructure contractor with a near-record $50 billion backlog. The company is the primary beneficiary of secular trends in grid modernization, renewable energy buildout, and data center electrification. Q1 2026 revenue grew 26% YoY to $7.87B with adjusted EPS of $2.68 (+51% YoY), both beating consensus by double digits. Valuation at ~35x forward earnings is elevated but justified by the backlog visibility and growth trajectory. The key risk is thin operating margins (~4%) which leave little room for error on tariffs, labor inflation, or project overruns.

drivers: Grid spending accelerates under federal infrastructure bills
Margins expand toward peer levels (6%+) as scale improves
Data center backlog grows 40%+ on hyperscaler demand
M&A continues to add accretive capabilities and revenue

risks: Tariffs and labor inflation compress already-thin margins
$6.4B debt load becomes burdensome if rates stay elevated
Valuation compression on any guidance miss

base_case.assumptions: Revenue grows 20–25% annually through 2027. Margins stabilize at 4.5–5%. Debt managed via operating cash flow. No equity raise needed.

key_risks: Thin operating margins (~4%) leave little buffer for cost inflation
$6.4B debt load is material if interest rates stay elevated
Fixed-price contract overruns are an industry hazard
Valuation at 35x forward earnings leaves no room for error
